***** After the hearing, the Court signs an order granting plaintiff's motion for preliminary injunction. (See signed order for complete ruling.) (302/RBU) *****

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Mar-24-2022 Continued To Apr-07-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Adam Cashman, Esq., SINGER CASHMAN LLP, (415) 500-6080, acashman@singercashman.com for Plaintiff Bonds Land Investment Co., Ltd.
No Other appearances.

Matthew Gluck, counsel for defendant Johnson Chan, contested the Court's tentative ruling, failed to appear for today's hearing.

No argument held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BONDS LAND INVESTMENT CP., LTD., A CALIFORNIA Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction

Plaintiff's motion for preliminary injunction is continued to April 7, 2022.

Plaintiff demonstrates that the mail service on defendant was proper. (See Denlinger v. Chinadotcom Corp. (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1396, 1405 [holding service of process by mail valid in Hong Kong]; Whyenlee Indus. Ltd. v. Superior Ct. (2019) 33 Cal.App.5th 364, 372-73; Wat Decl. (filed 3/14/22 and dated 3/11/22).) Plaintiff further demonstrates that defendant has sufficient minimum contacts with California to warrant the exercise of specific jurisdiction. (See Swenberg v. Dmarcian, Inc. (2021) 68 Cal.App.5th 280, 294 [jurisdiction present where (i) defendant purposefully availed himself of forum benefits, (ii) the controversy is related to or arises out of the defendant's contacts with the forum, and (iii) the assertion of personal jurisdiction would comport with fair play and substantial justice].) Defendant has purposefully availed himself of California benefits and has a substantial economic connection with this forum. The evidence demonstrates that defendant was a director of plaintiff, which is the managing director of Seaker & Sons, G.P. The parties dispute involves California property owned by Seaker & Sons. Despite being removed as a director and officer of plaintiff, defendant continues to attempt to act on its behalf in California. Defendant's conduct has even caused plaintiff's California bank accounts to be frozen. The alleged ultra vires California contacts are a basis for specific jurisdiction. (See Anson Chan Decl. (Filed 3/2/22); Complaint CPF-22-517696 [defendant's entity seeks to dissolve Seaker & Sons].)

The court continues this motion until April 7, 2022, to give defendant a chance to address the merits of the motion. By plaintiff's own admission, defendant was not properly served with the summons, complaint, and motion papers until March 10, 2022. (Wat Decl. (filed 3/14/22 and dated 3/11/22) pars. 3-5.) Given that late service, a continuance is warranted. Defendant to file and serve any supplemental opposition by April 1, 2022. Plaintiff to file and serve any supplemental reply by April 5, 2022 by noon.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com mailto:maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
